question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: endDate field] Can an unprivileged external user submit a crafted stream file whose embedded consensus timestamp is just before a configured endDate, causing the importer to halt via downloaderProperties.setEnabled(false) before processing a subsequent TOKENUNFREEZE transaction, permanently leaving the mirror node's token_account table with freeze_status=FROZEN and blocking all downstream fund transfers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: endBlockNumber field] Can an unprivileged external user, by observing that endBlockNumber is set to a block containing a TOKENFREEZE transaction but not the subsequent TOKENUNFREEZE block, cause the importer to permanently stop ingesting blocks at that boundary, leaving the mirror node's freeze state as FROZEN with no recovery path without operator intervention?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: startDate field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that startDate is nullable and has no lower-bound validation to craft a scenario where startDate is set to a timestamp after a TOKENUNFREEZE transaction but before a TOKENFREEZE transaction, causing the importer to skip the unfreeze record entirely and permanently record the account as FROZEN in the mirror node database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: startBlockNumber field] Can an unprivileged external user, given that startBlockNumber has only a @Min(-1) constraint with no upper-bound check, cause the importer to start ingestion at a block after a TOKENUNFREEZE transaction was recorded on-chain, resulting in the mirror node never persisting the unfreeze and permanently showing the account as FROZEN to all API consumers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getArchiveDestinationFolderPath(StreamFileData)] Can an unprivileged external user inject a stream file whose filename is fewer than 10 characters long, causing streamFileData.getFilename().substring(0, 10) to throw a StringIndexOutOfBoundsException that crashes the downloader thread, halting all stream processing and permanently freezing the mirror node's view of token freeze states at the point of the crash?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getArchiveDestinationFolderPath(StreamFileData)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream filename whose first 10 characters resolve to a path traversal sequence (e.g., '../../../etc'), causing getStreamPath().resolve(streamFileData.getFilename().substring(0, 10)) to write archive files outside the intended directory, corrupting the stream file chain and causing the importer to stall on hash verification, leaving TOKENUNFREEZE transactions unprocessed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getNetwork() uses StringUtils.substringBefore(this.network, '-') to cause a network value of 'demo-mainnet' to be parsed as 'demo', triggering isAllowAnonymousAccess() to return true and routing the importer to the demo bucket instead of mainnet, causing it to ingest stale or missing TOKENUNFREEZE transactions and permanently freezing funds in the mirror node's view?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that getNetwork() lowercases the network string and that hasCutover() uses case-sensitive MAINNET.equals(network) comparison, and craft a network value like 'MAINNET' that getNetwork() normalizes to 'mainnet' but where a downstream component using the raw network field bypasses cutover logic, causing block stream processing to skip TOKENUNFREEZE transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: HederaNetwork.hasCutover()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that hasCutover() only returns true for exactly 'mainnet' and 'testnet' strings, so that a network value of 'mainnet-2024' causes hasCutover() to return false, disabling block stream cutover logic and causing the importer to process record streams that omit TOKENUNFREEZE transactions, permanently freezing funds in the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: HederaNetwork.isAllowAnonymousAccess()] Can an unprivileged external user, by observing that isAllowAnonymousAccess() only returns true for 'demo', craft a network string 'demo-prod' that getNetwork() parses as 'demo', enabling anonymous access to a bucket that serves stale stream files missing TOKENUNFREEZE transactions, causing permanent freeze state in the mirror node database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: consensusMode field (ConsensusMode.EQUAL)] Can an unprivileged external user, given that ConsensusMode.EQUAL assigns stake=1 to all nodes regardless of actual stake, exploit a scenario where a minority of colluding nodes (each with equal weight) can reach the consensus threshold and inject a fake TOKENFREEZE stream file that is accepted by the importer, permanently freezing an account's token balance in the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: consensusMode field (ConsensusMode.STAKE)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit ConsensusMode.STAKE (which includes all nodes in stake calculation, not just address book nodes) to cause a node with artificially inflated stake to dominate consensus verification, accepting a fraudulent TOKENFREEZE stream file that permanently freezes a user's token account in the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: consensusMode field (ConsensusMode.STAKE_IN_ADDRESS_BOOK)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the STAKE_IN_ADDRESS_BOOK mode's behavior of excluding nodes not in the address book from the total stake calculation, causing the effective consensus threshold to be lower than expected, allowing fewer colluding nodes to reach consensus on a fraudulent TOKENFREEZE stream file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: nodePublicKey field] Can an unprivileged external user, given that nodePublicKey overrides all address book node public keys when set, exploit a misconfiguration where nodePublicKey is set to a publicly known test key, causing the importer to accept stream files signed by that key including fraudulent TOKENFREEZE transactions that permanently freeze user funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: nodePublicKey field] Can an unprivileged external user observe that nodePublicKey has no format validation constraint (no @Pattern or @NotNull), submit a zero-length or whitespace-only nodePublicKey value that causes signature verification to silently fail or be skipped, allowing any stream file including fraudulent TOKENFREEZE records to be accepted and permanently freeze token accounts?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: initialAddressBook field] Can an unprivileged external user, given that initialAddressBook has no validation constraint and accepts any Path value, exploit a path pointing to a malicious address book file that lists attacker-controlled nodes with high stake, causing the importer to accept fraudulent TOKENFREEZE stream files signed by attacker keys and permanently freeze user token accounts?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: initialAddressBook field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that initialAddressBook is nullable with no existence check at property binding time, causing the importer to fall back to a stale or empty address book that accepts a lower consensus threshold, enabling fraudulent TOKENFREEZE stream files to pass verification and permanently freeze token accounts in the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: importHistoricalAccountInfo field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that importHistoricalAccountInfo=true causes HistoricalAccountInfoMigration to run and set entity.deleted=true for accounts marked deleted in the pre-reset snapshot, causing accounts that were unfrozen after the reset to appear permanently deleted/frozen in the mirror node's entity table?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: importHistoricalAccountInfo field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between importHistoricalAccountInfo=true and startDate being set after EXPORT_DATE (2019-09-14T00:00:10Z), causing the historical account info migration to be silently skipped, leaving accounts without proper key/expiry data that causes downstream freeze-status queries to return incorrect FROZEN states?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: dataPath field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that dataPath defaults to Paths.get('.', 'data') (a relative path) and has no absolute-path validation, causing the importer to resolve stream files from an attacker-controlled working directory containing crafted stream files with fraudulent TOKENFREEZE transactions that permanently freeze user token accounts?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: streamPath (lazy getter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Getter(lazy=true) behavior of streamPath, which is computed once from dataPath at first access, to cause a race condition where dataPath is changed after streamPath is initialized, causing the importer to read stream files from the old path while writing archives to the new path, breaking the hash chain and halting processing of TOKENUNFREEZE transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: groupByDay field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the groupByDay=true default, which calls streamFileData.getFilename().substring(0, 10) to extract a date prefix, by injecting a stream file with a filename that starts with a valid 10-character date string but contains path traversal characters in positions 0-9 (e.g., '..%2F..%2F..'), causing archive writes to corrupt the stream directory and halt TOKENUNFREEZE processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: groupByDay field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the transition from groupByDay=true to groupByDay=false (or vice versa) mid-operation, causing the archive destination path to change and the importer to lose track of previously archived stream files, breaking the hash chain verification and causing the importer to stall before processing TOKENUNFREEZE transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: smartContractThrottlingVersion field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that smartContractThrottlingVersion defaults to '0.69.0' and is used to select gas calculation logic, by submitting contract transactions with a HAPI version just below 0.69.0 that triggers legacy gas calculation, causing incorrect gas consumed values that make contract-based token unfreeze calls appear to fail, leaving token accounts permanently frozen in the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: smartContractThrottlingVersion field] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Version.parse() parsing of smartContractThrottlingVersion with a malformed version string (e.g., '0.69.0.0.0' or '0.69') that causes Version comparison to behave unexpectedly, causing all contract transactions to use the wrong gas calculation path and making contract-based TOKENUNFREEZE calls appear unsuccessful, permanently freezing token accounts?
